heading: Study in Australia
tagline: Programs, costs, intakes, and what to prepare — explained plainly.
note: Figures below are general public estimates (2024/2025 range) meant to set expectations, not quotes. They change over time and by institution — Sabina confirms exact numbers per student during assessment.

programAndDuration.heading: Program Types & Duration
programAndDuration.paragraph: Australian tertiary education is split into Vocational Education & Training (VET) and Higher Education (HE), ranked Level 1–10 on the Australian Qualifications Framework (AQF).

livingExpenseTable.heading: 1st-Year Living Cost Estimate (Department of Home Affairs guideline)

intakes.heading: Intakes
intakes.paragraph: Most Australian universities offer two main intakes each year:
intakes.note: A few universities also run a smaller intake around October/November, and some use a trimester system (February, June, September) instead.

oshc.heading: Overseas Student Health Cover (OSHC)
oshc.paragraph: Every student visa holder is required to have OSHC for the duration of their stay. Sabina helps students understand which cover level and provider suits their circumstances.

visaFeeEstimate.heading: Student Visa (Subclass 500) Fee Estimate
visaFeeEstimate.note: A card-payment surcharge of roughly 1.4% typically applies on top of these base fees.

deadlines.heading: Application Timing
deadlines.paragraph: University and visa processing together can take months. Sabina recommends starting the process at least 6 months before your target intake — up to 8 months for competitive or specialised courses.
